title: Going Back Up
subtitle: Navigate up with cd ..

sections.content: You can go into folders — but how do you go back? Two dots (`..`) is the universal shortcut for **the parent folder** (the one above you).
sections.analogy: Going into a folder is like walking into a room. `cd ..` is walking back out the door you came in through.
keyPoints: You can chain them: `cd ../..` goes up two levels

sections.instruction: You're inside documents. How do you go back up to your home folder?
sections.prompt: Type `cd ..` to go up one folder. `..` always means 'parent folder'.

onSuccess.message: You're back in `/home/user`. `cd ..` is like clicking the back button.

sections.instruction: Go up two levels to get back home. You can chain it.
sections.prompt: Type `cd ../..` to go up two folders at once.

onSuccess.message: Back to `/home/user`. Each `..` goes up one level, so `../..` goes up two.

sections.prompt: To go up one directory level, you type: cd ___

completionMessage: You can now go up with `cd ..` and chain them with `cd ../..`. You're never stuck in a deep folder.
